title: Pete Docter Says Pixar Cut LGBTQ Storyline From ‘Elio’ Because ‘We’re Making a Movie, Not Hundreds of Millions of Dollars of Therapy’
content:
ByJack Dunn

Pete Docter, chief creative officer atPixarand director of “Monsters Inc.,” “Up” and “Inside Out,” recently told theWall Street Journalwhy the studio decided to completely overhaul 2025’s “Elio” and cut out the film’s LGBTQ storyline.

Docter told the WSJ that Pixar didn’t want to expose its young audience to things they weren’t ready to see or hadn’t discussed with their parents. He said, “We’re making a movie, not hundreds of millions of dollars of therapy.”

“Elio” follows a lonely kid who looks to the stars for friends after being rejected as an outsider by other kids his age. Despite the clever premise, WSJ reports that the film tested poorly in early screenings, with most audiences saying they wouldn’t pay to see it in theaters. In response, Docter ordered a complete overhaul of the film, even though a significant chunk of the animation was complete. The overhaul led to the exit of the film’s original director, Adrian Molina.

Madeline Sharafian and Domee Shi then stepped in and made significant changes to “Elio.” This included, according to the WSJ, removing moments that indicated the title character was gay. Previous versions of the film reportedly showed Elio with a pink bike and had a scene where he imagines a life together with his male crush. The changes sparked backlash within the Pixar staff, drama that was compounded by Disney’s decision tocut a transgender characterfrom Pixar’s animated series “Win or Lose.”

“Elio” launched in June 2025, earning $150 million worldwide at the end of its run. That would be a stellar number if it weren’t for the fact that the film cost $150 million to produce, not factoring in global marketing costs.
title_zh: 皮特·道克特(Pete Docter)表示，皮克斯(Pixar)在《艾利奧》(Elio)中刪除了LGBTQ情節，因為「我們在製作一部電影，而不是數億美元的治療」。
content_zh:
由Jack Dunn報導

皮克斯(Pixar)的首席創意官彼得·道克特(Pete Docter)最近在《華爾街日報》(Wall Street Journal)中解釋了為什麼該工作室決定徹底重塑2025年的電影《艾利奧》(Elio)，並刪除了影片中的LGBTQ故事情節。

道克特表示，皮克斯不想讓年輕觀眾接觸他們尚未準備好看到或尚未與父母討論的內容。他說：“我們是在製作電影，而不是花幾億美元進行治療。”

《艾利奧》講述了一個孤獨的孩子，在被同齡人拒絕後，向星空尋找朋友。儘管這個創意聽起來不錯，《華爾街日報》報導該片在早期放映中的測試反應不佳，大多數觀眾表示不會付費在電影院觀看。作為回應，道克特下令對影片進行全面重塑，儘管動畫的相當一部分已經完成。這次重塑導致了影片原導演阿德里安·莫利納(Adrian Molina)的離開。

隨後，瑪德琳·沙拉菲安(Madeline Sharafian)和史蒂米·施(Domee Shi)接手，對《艾利奧》進行了重大修改。根據《華爾街日報》的報導，這些修改包括刪除暗示主角是同性戀的片段。先前版本的影片中，艾利奧曾騎著粉紅色的自行車，並有一個場景是他幻想與他的男性暗戀對象共度生活。這些變更在皮克斯內部引發了反彈，這場戲劇因迪士尼決定刪除皮克斯動畫系列《贏或輸》(Win or Lose)中的一個跨性別角色而更加劇。

《艾利奧》於2025年6月上映，最終在全球賺取了1.5億美元。如果不考慮全球市場推廣成本，這將是一個驚人的數字，但該片的製作成本也達到了1.5億美元。
summary_zh:
由Jack Dunn報導

皮克斯(Pixar)的首席創意官彼得·道克特(Pete Docter)最近在《華爾街日報》(Wall Street Journal)中解釋了為什麼該工作室決定徹底重塑2025年的電影《艾利奧》(Elio)，並刪除了影片中的LGBTQ故事情節。

道克特表示，皮克斯不想讓年輕觀眾接觸他們尚未準備好看到或尚未與父母討論的內容。
